event_blocks.summary: The performance commences in a black and white studio environment, with subtle sounds of footsteps and rustling, before transitioning to full color. On-screen text identifies the group as 'Pitch Slapped' and the event as the '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella,' with the Berklee College of Music logo visible. A male voice announces the broadcast on 'Pitch Slapped Radio Live,' followed by a rhythmic a cappella vocal percussion intro. The Male Lead Performer, in a white shirt, black tie, and suspenders, and the Female Lead Performer, in a black dress with a statement necklace, deliver passionate lead vocals and expressive gestures, singing lines such as "Do you mind if we take our time? 'Cause I've been here before and it nearly took my life," "this song will keep you grooving," "What are you gonna do when the world don't orbit around you," and "Don't go crying to your mama, 'cause you're on your own in the real world." The full Group of Performers, dressed in coordinated outfits, provides rich harmonies, intricate vocal arrangements, and synchronized dance moves, including spins, coordinated arm gestures, and dynamic footwork, creating an energetic and theatrical soundscape. The Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt emerges as a prominent focal point, smiling broadly and gesturing expressively, particularly during sections like "ain't it fine" and "ain't it good to be on your own." The studio environment features wooden floors, white walls, arched doorways, and a large round clock. The performance includes a solo by the Female Lead Performer singing a rapid, rhythmic line, "I'll take me out, I'll take me out, I'll take back that, I'll take back that, I'll take me out," and spoken interjections from the Male Lead Performer like "Come on y'all," "Pitch Slapped," and "Let's party." The piece culminates in a powerful, unified finale with sustained vocalizations and rhythmic "push, pull" sounds, maintaining high energy and cohesion.

On-Screen Text.description: White text overlay reading 'Pitch Slapped' and '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella' appears at the start, identifying the group and event, then fades out as the performance progresses.

Berklee College of Music Logo.description: A red square logo with white text 'Berklee college of music' displayed in the bottom right corner throughout the video, serving as a consistent visual identifier.

Studio Environment.description: A spacious rehearsal room with wooden floors, white walls, arched doorways, and a large round clock on the wall, serving as the performance backdrop with consistent natural lighting.

Female Lead Performer.description: A young woman in a black dress with a prominent statement necklace, who performs with synchronized movements and expressive hand gestures alongside the male lead, frequently interacting with him and the camera, and taking a distinct lead vocal section with a clear, melodic voice.

Group of Performers.description: A mixed-gender ensemble dressed in coordinated outfits—men in white shirts, black ties, and suspenders; women in black tops or dresses with varied accessories—performing synchronized dance and vocal movements, maintaining tight formation and high energy, providing rich a cappella harmonies, rhythmic vocalizations, and beatboxing throughout the segment, forming the instrumental and backing vocal foundation.

Male Lead Performer.description: A young man in a white shirt, black tie, and suspenders, who sings expressively and leads the choreography with dynamic arm gestures and facial expressions, often positioned at the front of the group, serving as the primary lead vocalist with a strong tenor voice, delivering most of the song's lyrics and performing vocal runs and ad-libs.

Female Performer in Black Top and White.description: A young woman with blonde hair wearing a black top and a white skirt, who becomes a prominent focal point in the latter half of the video, smiling and gesturing expressively during the performance, often positioned near the front.

raw_segments: The video opens in black and white, accompanied by subtle sounds of footsteps and rustling. On-screen text identifies the group as 'Pitch Slapped' and the event as the '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella,' with the Berklee College of Music logo visible. A male voice then announces, "Hello, folks. Thank you for tuning into Pitch Slapped Radio Live," before a rhythmic a cappella vocal percussion intro begins. A male lead performer, dressed in a white shirt, black tie, and suspenders, sings passionately with expressive gestures, delivering the lines, "Do you mind if we take our time? 'Cause I've been here before and it nearly took my life." A female lead performer beside him, in a black dress and statement necklace, mirrors his energy with expressive hand movements. As the black-and-white filter transitions to full color, revealing the wooden floor, arched doorways, and a large clock of the rehearsal studio, the full group of performers joins in, providing rich harmonies and synchronized dance moves, including spins and coordinated arm gestures. The male lead continues to deliver the main melody, supported by the ensemble's intricate vocal arrangements and rhythmic backing, creating an energetic and theatrical performance that concludes with more vocal percussion.

The segment opens in a brightly lit, spacious rehearsal room with wooden floors and white walls, featuring arched doorways and a large round clock. A mixed-gender ensemble, the Group of Performers, dressed in coordinated outfits—men in white shirts, black ties, and suspenders; women in black tops or dresses with varied accessories—executes dynamic choreography. The Male Lead Performer, in a white shirt, black tie, and suspenders, and the Female Lead Performer, in a black dress with a prominent statement necklace, take center stage, leading the group with synchronized dance and vocal expressions. Throughout, the Berklee College of Music Logo is visible in the bottom right. The audio features a lively, rhythmic vocalization of 'la-la-la' from the group, creating a vibrant, almost percussive musical backdrop. As the performance progresses, the camera pans and zooms to capture close-ups of individual performers, including a young woman with blonde hair in a black top and white skirt, who becomes a focal point, smiling and gesturing expressively. The atmosphere is joyful, with performers laughing and engaging with each other, their enthusiastic 'la-la-la's continuing to drive the energetic performance.

The minute opens with a vibrant, high-energy a cappella performance by a mixed-gender ensemble in a spacious rehearsal studio with wooden floors and white walls. The Group of Performers, dressed in coordinated outfits, execute synchronized dance moves and vocal harmonies, delivering a professionally produced a cappella track featuring prominent lead vocals, intricate backing harmonies, and dynamic vocal percussion. The Female Lead Performer, in a black dress and statement necklace, takes center stage with expressive gestures and a powerful lead vocal, singing lines like "this song will keep you grooving" and "this song's the one." The Male Lead Performer, in a white shirt and suspenders, also delivers a strong lead vocal and dynamic choreography. As the camera pans across the group, highlighting their energetic movements and joyful expressions, the Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt becomes a prominent focal point, smiling broadly and gesturing expressively as she dances, contributing to the ensemble's sound. The Berklee College of Music Logo remains visible in the bottom right corner, anchoring the performance to the institution, while the Studio Environment with its arched doorways and a large round clock provides a clean, professional backdrop. Towards the end of the minute, the male lead vocal takes prominence, singing "I don't mind letting you down easy, but just give it time. If it don't hurt now, just wait, just wait a while," maintaining the energetic a cappella style. The group transitions through various formations, including circular and staggered lines, maintaining tight synchronization and enthusiasm, culminating in a final, unified pose that emphasizes their cohesion and joy, all set to their powerful vocal performance.

The video opens with a dynamic a cappella performance by a group of young performers in a spacious rehearsal studio. The Male Lead Performer, dressed in a white shirt and suspenders, immediately takes a prominent vocal role, delivering rhythmic and melodic lines like "You're not the big fish in the pond no more," while executing dynamic choreography. He is soon joined by the Female Lead Performer, in a black dress, who shares lead vocals with a powerful, melodic voice, particularly during the catchy chorus, "What are you gonna do when the world don't orbit around you." The entire Group of Performers, dressed in coordinated outfits, provides rich, synchronized harmonies and intricate vocal percussion, creating a full, energetic soundscape that drives their fluid, synchronized dance moves. As the camera pans, capturing their joyful expressions and precise arm movements, the Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt emerges as a visual focal point, smiling broadly and contributing to the group's vibrant vocal tapestry, especially during the "ain't it fine" and "ain't it good to be on your own" sections. The atmosphere is celebratory, with bright lighting highlighting their movements, all while the Berklee College of Music Logo remains visible, anchoring this exuberant display of musical unity and precision.

The video opens in a spacious rehearsal studio with wooden floors and white walls, featuring arched doorways and a large round clock, all bathed in natural lighting. On-screen text 'Pitch Slapped' and '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella' appears briefly before fading. A group of young performers, dressed in coordinated outfits—men in white shirts, black ties, and suspenders; women in black tops or dresses—execute dynamic, synchronized dance moves and powerful a cappella vocalizations. The Male Lead Performer and Female Lead Performer take center stage, leading the choreography with expressive arm gestures and facial expressions, singing the repeated chorus, "Don't go crying to your mama, 'cause you're on your own in the real world." The camera fluidly captures close-ups and wide shots, emphasizing the group’s energy and cohesion. Around the 0:41 mark, the song shifts, and the Female Lead Performer takes a solo, singing a rapid, rhythmic line, "I'll take me out, I'll take me out, I'll take back that, I'll take back that, I'll take me out," while the group provides vocal percussion and harmonies. The Male Lead Performer then interjects with spoken words, "Come on y'all," "Pitch Slapped," and "Let's party," before the group launches into an energetic vocal percussion and harmony section. The Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt becomes a prominent focal point in the latter half, smiling and gesturing expressively, contributing to the lively and joyful atmosphere. The Berklee College of Music Logo remains visible in the bottom right corner throughout, anchoring the performance to the institution, as the performers laugh and engage directly with the camera, creating an immersive and celebratory experience.

The video opens in a spacious, well-lit rehearsal studio with wooden floors and white walls, featuring the Berklee College of Music Logo consistently visible in the bottom right corner, and brief on-screen text identifying 'Pitch Slapped' and the '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella' before fading. A dynamic a cappella performance immediately begins with the Group of Performers providing rhythmic vocalizations and beatboxing, creating a full, percussive soundscape. The Male Lead Performer, in a white shirt, black tie, and suspenders, takes center stage, singing lead with expressive facial gestures and energetic arm movements, delivering lyrics like "There's not too many, no one but you in crowded rooms, we can do anything" in a strong tenor voice. He is joined by the Female Lead Performer, in a black dress with a statement necklace, who mirrors his energy with synchronized dance moves and expressive hand gestures, while the rest of the group, dressed in coordinated outfits, executes tight, synchronized dance routines and provides rich harmonies. The camera alternates between close-ups of the leads and wide shots of the ensemble, capturing their dynamic footwork and rhythmic clapping. Around the 0:35 mark, the Female Lead Performer takes over lead vocals, singing "And you got a little love in your life. They don't have to be running around and around and around" with a clear, melodic voice, while the Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt becomes increasingly prominent, smiling and gesturing expressively near the front. The Male Lead Performer then returns to lead vocals, delivering expressive lines such as "I won't leave your love astray" with vocal runs and ad-libs, before the group collectively builds to a powerful, unified finale with sustained vocalizations and rhythmic "push, pull" sounds, maintaining high energy throughout the consistent studio backdrop with its arched doorways and large round clock.

event_blocks.summary: In a spacious rehearsal studio, the Group of Performers, identified by on-screen text as 'Pitch Slapped' from the '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella,' delivered a high-energy a cappella performance, executing synchronized dance moves with dynamic arm gestures, rhythmic vocal percussion, and strong harmonies. The Male Lead Performer took center stage, singing passionately with a clear, energetic voice, delivering lines like "fill me up until there's nothing left" and "let us take back tonight," while leading choreography with sharp, wide arm extensions, mirrored by the Female Lead Performer in her black dress and statement necklace. The Berklee College of Music Logo was visible in the bottom right as the camera panned across the energetic group. Transitioning into a structured circle formation with the Male Lead Performer at the center, he delivered the line, "You gon' mess around and find out there ain't no one better," supported by unified backing vocals, while the Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt gestured toward the camera. Around 0:39, the performance shifted dramatically as the Male Lead Performer began a melancholic, soulful solo, singing "I am feeling so small... I know nothing at all," accompanied by sparse, soft, sustained vocal harmonies from the group, creating a stark emotional contrast.

The video opens in a bright, professional studio environment, a spacious rehearsal room with wooden floors, white walls, a large round clock above a fireplace, and arched doorways. The Berklee College of Music logo is consistently displayed in the bottom right corner. Initially, soft, sustained vocalizations create an atmospheric opening, accompanied by on-screen text identifying the group 'Pitch Slapped' and the '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella'. The Male Lead Performer and Female Lead Performer stand center stage, facing each other, as the Group of Performers forms a semi-circle behind them. A male voice then distinctly sings "Say something." The Male Lead Performer initiates choreography with expressive hand gestures and facial expressions, mirrored precisely by the Female Lead Performer. As upbeat, rhythmic a cappella music begins with strong vocal percussion and a bassline, a female voice takes the lead, singing catchy lines like "Come on over, come on over baby." The group transitions into dynamic, synchronized dance moves, with the Male Lead Performer stepping forward and the Female Lead Performer moving to his side, continuing her lead vocals with lyrics such as "Hey boy, don't you know I got something going on." The camera pans across the ensemble, highlighting their energy and coordination. The performers are dressed in coordinated outfits—men in white shirts, black ties, and suspenders; women in black tops or dresses with varied accessories. Midway through, the focus shifts to the Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt, who becomes a prominent focal point, smiling and gesturing expressively as she moves to the front, contributing to the vibrant group performance. The choreography intensifies with sharp, rhythmic steps and arm movements, maintaining tight formation. The female lead vocals continue with lines like "I know you know, so baby don't pretend you want to keep me guessing if you..." and "Don't wanna play this game with you baby," supported by the group's harmonious backing vocals, vocal percussion, and bassline, conveying a strong sense of unity and performance excellence throughout the energetic atmosphere. The natural lighting remains consistent, enhancing the scene's clarity.

The video opens with the On-Screen Text 'Pitch Slapped' and '2014 International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella' setting the stage for a dynamic a cappella performance by a group of young performers from Berklee College of Music, whose logo remains visible in the bottom right. In a spacious rehearsal studio with wooden floors and white walls, the Female Lead Performer, in a white top and black pants, takes center stage, singing the lead 'You text me, saying you want me for back, please' with expressive gestures and energetic movements. The Group of Performers, in coordinated outfits, provides intricate backing harmonies and rhythmic vocalizations, executing synchronized dance moves. As the Female Lead continues her powerful vocal delivery, 'But what you waiting for? It's open house, why don't you take a tour?', the camera captures various angles, highlighting their theatrical expressions. The Male Lead Performer, in a white shirt and suspenders, joins in, mirroring her movements and adding vocal intensity as the ensemble transitions into a powerful chorus, with voices blending on lines like 'I've been waiting for you' and 'Give it to you.' The Female Lead then returns to a melodic lead, 'But I won't have to wonder, baby,' before the Male Lead takes over with a distinct lead vocal, 'What could you drive me crazy? Take me for a ride.' The performance maintains high energy, with the Female Lead again singing, 'But I won't have to wonder, if you just come on over.' Finally, a Female Performer in a black top and white skirt, who has become increasingly prominent, delivers a clear, enthusiastic lead line, 'Don't you want to be the one tonight?', as the group concludes with a final rhythmic vocalization.

event_blocks.summary: In a spacious, well-lit rehearsal studio with wooden floors and white walls, the Female Lead Performer, clad in a white top and black pants with a statement necklace, initiates a dynamic a cappella performance, leading choreography with expressive hand gestures and vocal delivery, singing "Don't you want to be just you and me?" and "We can do what comes naturally." The Male Lead Performer, dressed in a white shirt, black tie, and suspenders, joins with animated facial expressions and gestures, interacting with the Female Lead Performer amidst the energetic vocal arrangement. The Group of Performers, in coordinated attire—men in white shirts, black ties, and suspenders; women in black tops or patterned pants—execute synchronized, high-energy dance moves, forming tight formations while delivering powerful harmonies and vocal percussion, chanting "What a girl likes, what a girl wants" and the core lyric "All I want is you." The Berklee College of Music Logo is visible in the bottom right corner throughout. Close-ups highlight the Female Performer in Black Top and White Skirt, who becomes a focal point with her smiling, expressive gestures, singing along to the escalating intensity of "All I want is you." The performance culminates with the group transitioning into a final pose, pointing at the camera, before the screen fades to black displaying on-screen text options: 'Credits', 'Subscribe', and 'Watch more from Berklee', concluding the a cappella piece.

event_blocks.summary: The video transitions from a live performance to a closing credits sequence, accompanied by a continuous, upbeat instrumental pop/rock music track. Initially, three small video thumbnails appear at the top of the screen, showcasing diverse musical performances: a woman singing into a microphone with a 'bim' logo, a man singing into a vintage-style microphone on stage, and hands playing a keyboard. Below these, white text boxes reading 'Credits', 'Subscribe', and 'Watch more from Berklee serve as a call-to-action, all underscored by the energetic music. The thumbnails then cycle through various performers, including a woman singing with a man in a hat, a man singing on stage under a spotlight, and a drummer playing in a dimly lit setting. The scene subsequently fades to black, and white text credits begin to roll, listing song titles such as 'Take Our Time', 'Radio Song', 'Ain't it Fun', and 'Take Back the Night', along with lead vocalists and arrangers. Additional credits follow, naming the members of 'Pitch Slapped' by vocal range, choreographers, recording engineers, videographer, and mastering studio. The Berklee College of Music logo is no longer visible as the environment shifts entirely to a black screen with the rolling text, marking the end of the performance and the beginning of the closing sequence, with the instrumental music providing a consistent, lively backdrop.
